title: chaenorhinum origanifolium subsp. cadevallii
intro: Chaenorhinum origanifolium subsp. cadevallii, also known as the Balearic shell gentian, is a rare and endemic subspecies of flowering plant found in the Balearic Islands, specifically in the island of Mallorca. It is a succulent plant with medicinal properties, primarily used to treat digestive issues and inflammation. The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of gastritis, ulcers, and diarrhea, as well as to reduce fever and relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of the Balearic shell gentian include tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to create teas, salves, and other medicinal products.
